subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Audit
- **Supported counts** — 2 to 4 players with a single set.
- **Solo mode** — Not included in the base game.
- **Character packs** — Optional expansions add asymmetric starting decks (Fighter, Ranger, Cleric, Wizard, Thief).

#### Cards
- **Market Deck** — 80 market cards (actions and champions across 4 factions).
- **Fire Gem pile** — 10 Fire Gem cards (always available to purchase, similar to Explorers in Star Realms).
- **Gold cards** — Starting currency cards providing 1 Gold each.
- **Dagger cards** — Starting combat cards providing 1 Combat each.
- **Ruby cards** — Starting cards providing 2 Gold each.

#### Factions
- **Imperial (yellow)** — Authority gain, card draw, and healing.
- **Necros (purple)** — Sacrifice and discard effects, graveyard manipulation.
- **Wild (green)** — High Combat damage and direct aggression.
- **Guild (blue)** — Trade generation and versatile economy.

#### Tokens
- **Score cards** — Authority tracking cards for each player.
- **Sacrifice tokens** — Used with certain champion abilities.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Table Setup
1. **Market Deck** — Shuffle all 80 market cards into a face-down deck.
2. **Market Row** — Reveal 5 cards face up to form the Market Row.
3. **Fire Gem pile** — Place the 10 Fire Gems face up in a separate stack.
4. **Sacrifice pile** — Designate an area for sacrificed cards (removed from game).
5. **Player areas** — Each player needs space for draw deck, discard pile, champions in play, and hand area.

--- 3-4 Players ---
- **Multiple targets** — With more players, choose which opponent to attack each time you deal Combat damage.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Standard Starting Deck (No Character Pack)
- **Starting deck** — Each player takes 10 cards: 7 Gold cards, 1 Dagger card, 1 Ruby card, and 1 Short Sword card.
- **Shuffle** — Shuffle your 10-card starting deck.
- **Starting hand** — Draw 3 cards for the first turn (5 cards from the second turn onward).
- **Starting Authority** — Set Authority to 50.

#### Character Pack Setup (Optional)
- **Replace starting deck** — If using a Character Pack, replace the generic starting deck with that character's unique 10-card deck.
- **Character ability** — Each character has a unique ability card placed in play at the start.
- **Adjusted Authority** — Some characters start with different Authority totals as printed on their character card.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### First Turn
- **Determine first player** — Choose randomly.
- **Reduced first hand** — First player draws only 3 cards.
- **Second player** — Second player also draws 3 cards on first turn (in 2-player).
- **Normal draws** — From turn 2 onward, draw 5 cards at end of turn.
- **Begin play** — Alternate turns clockwise.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Turn Sequence
1. **Main Phase** — Play cards from hand, use champion abilities, activate ally abilities, acquire cards, attack opponents.
2. **Discard Phase** — Discard all played action cards and remaining hand cards.
3. **Draw Phase** — Draw 5 new cards from your deck.

#### Main Phase Details
- **Play freely** — Play any number of cards in any order.
- **Pool resources** — Gold and Combat accumulate during your turn.
- **Spend anytime** — Buy cards or deal damage at any point during your Main Phase.
- **Champions persist** — Champions remain in play between turns until stunned or sacrificed.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Acquiring Cards
- **Buy from Market** — Pay a card's Gold cost to acquire it into your discard pile.
- **Buy Fire Gems** — Pay 2 Gold to acquire a Fire Gem (provides 2 Gold or can be sacrificed for 3 Combat).
- **Market refill** — Replace purchased cards immediately from the Market Deck.

#### Dealing Damage
- **Attack opponent** — Spend Combat to reduce an opponent's Authority.
- **Stun champions** — Spend Combat equal to a champion's defense to stun it (turn it sideways).
- **Guard priority** — If an opponent has a Guard champion in play, you must stun all Guards before attacking the player or non-Guard champions.

#### Card Types
- **Actions** — Play for immediate effect, discard at end of turn.
- **Champions** — Play to your champion area, remain in play until stunned.
- **Guard champions** — Champions with the Guard keyword that must be stunned before attacking the player.

#### Champion Abilities
- **Expend ability** — Turn the champion sideways to use its expend ability (once per turn, ready at start of your turn).
- **Ally ability** — Triggers when you have another card of the same faction in play.
- **Sacrifice ability** — Remove the champion from the game for a powerful one-time effect.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Faction Ally System
- **Trigger condition** — Playing a card while another card of the same faction is already in play triggers ally abilities.
- **Champions count** — Champions in play from previous turns count for ally triggers.
- **Multiple factions** — Some cards belong to multiple factions.

#### Stunning and Recovery
- **Stun effect** — A stunned champion is turned sideways and its owner puts it in their discard pile at the start of their next turn.
- **Prepare step** — At the start of your turn, ready all your champions (turn them upright) and discard any stunned champions.

#### Sacrifice Mechanic
- **Permanent removal** — Sacrificed cards are removed from the game entirely.
- **Strategic thinning** — Sacrifice weak starting cards to improve deck quality.
- **Fire Gem sacrifice** — Fire Gems can be sacrificed for 3 Combat as a burst damage option.

--- 3-4 Players ---
#### Multiplayer Rules
- **Target choice** — When dealing Combat, choose which opponent(s) to target.
- **Split damage** — You may split Combat across multiple opponents.
- **Politics** — Multiplayer adds negotiation and threat assessment.
- **Elimination** — Eliminated players are out; play continues until one player remains.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End Trigger
- **Authority depletion** — A player is eliminated when their Authority reaches 0 or below.

--- 2 Players ---
- **Immediate end** — Game ends when one player's Authority hits 0.

--- 3-4 Players ---
- **Elimination continues** — Eliminated players leave the game; play continues until one player remains.

#### Win Condition
- **Last standing** — The last player with Authority remaining wins.
- **No VP tally** — Pure elimination game with no point scoring.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Focus 1-2 factions** — Concentrate purchases in 1-2 factions to reliably trigger ally abilities.
- **Champions early** — Champions provide recurring value every turn and act as shields with Guard.
- **Thin aggressively** — Use sacrifice effects to remove weak Gold and Dagger cards.
- **Economy first** — Build Gold generation in the first few turns to afford powerful 5-7 cost cards.
- **Watch for synergy** — Cards that draw additional cards create explosive turns.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Ignoring Guard champions** — Leaving opponent Guards alive lets them absorb your Combat every turn.
- **Faction sprawl** — Buying from all 4 factions dilutes ally trigger frequency.
- **Neglecting deck thinning** — A thick deck full of starting cards draws weak hands in the mid-game.
- **Wasting Combat** — Unused Combat disappears at end of turn; always spend it on something.
- **Forgetting champion expend abilities** — Champions ready at the start of your turn; always check if an expend ability is worth using.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Aggression vs Defense
- **Rush damage** — Focus on Combat and direct attacks to pressure opponent's Authority.
- **Build defense** — Invest in Guard champions and Authority gain to outlast the opponent.

#### Decision 2: When to Sacrifice
- **Early sacrifice** — Thin weak cards immediately for better future draws.
- **Hold for value** — Keep a champion's ongoing ability until the sacrifice effect would be game-deciding.

#### Decision 3: Target Selection (3-4 Players)
- **Focus fire** — Concentrate on eliminating one opponent to reduce threats.
- **Spread pressure** — Keep all opponents low to prevent anyone from building momentum.
